evidence: Statement quantifies over polynomials of degree n with coefficients in {-1,1}, i.e. Littlewood polynomials.
verify_note: Decl exists (research open); degree-n polys with coeffs in {-1,1} are exactly Littlewood polynomials; QID label matches.

evidence: The bound is on max_{|z|=1} |P(z)|, the maximum modulus over the unit circle (Metric.sphere 0 1 in the Lean statement).
verify_note: Decl exists; sup over Metric.sphere 0 1 = max over the unit circle, load-bearing in the bound; QID matches.

evidence: The problem asks whether the cardinal partition relation 2^lambda -> (kappa_alpha+1)^{r+1} implies lambda -> (kappa_alpha)^r, a question of Erdos-Hajnal-Rado partition calculus.
verify_note: Decl exists; statement is cardinal partition calculus, whose canonical Wikipedia/Wikidata home is infinitary combinatorics (Partition calculus redirects there); QID matches.

evidence: Docstring: 'Generalises the classical Erdős–Rado stepping-up/down theorem for pairs.'
verify_note: Decl Erdos1167.erdos_1167.variants.r_eq_two exists (research open); docstring explicitly cites the Erdos-Rado stepping-up/down theorem; QID matches.

evidence: Asks whether every graph with chromatic number lambda contains a triangle-free subgraph with chromatic number kappa (via SimpleGraph.chromaticCardinal).
verify_note: Decl exists; chromaticCardinal (cardinal chromatic number) is the central quantity of the statement; QID matches.

evidence: The sought subgraph H must satisfy H.coe.CliqueFree 3, i.e. be triangle-free.
verify_note: Decl exists; H.coe.CliqueFree 3 = triangle-free, the defining property of the sought subgraph; QID matches.

evidence: Hypothesis is a graph G with chromatic number aleph_1 (G.chromaticCardinal = aleph 1).
verify_note: Decl exists; hypothesis G.chromaticCardinal = aleph 1 makes chromatic number load-bearing; QID matches.

evidence: Asks for a colouring of the edges with aleph_1 many colours such that some vertex colour class contains all edge colours.
verify_note: Q1050972/enwiki 'Edge coloring' denotes PROPER edge coloring (chromatic index); the statement's c_edge is an arbitrary assignment of aleph_1 colours with no properness constraint - different concept.

evidence: The z_i satisfy |z_i| = 1 and M_n = max_{|z|=1} |p_n(z)| is taken over the unit circle.
verify_note: Decl Erdos119.erdos_119.parts.i exists (research solved); |z_i|=1 and M_n = sup over ||w||=1 make the unit circle load-bearing; QID matches.

evidence: Asks for a basis A of order r (f_r(n) > 0 for all large n) with sum_{n<=x} f_r(n)^2 << x; erdosproblems.com tags it 'additive basis'.
verify_note: Decl exists; 'basis A of order r' with f_r(n)>0 for all large n is exactly an (asymptotic) additive basis; QID matches.

evidence: Docstring: 'Erdős and Rényi proved by the probabilistic method that there exists a set A ...'.
verify_note: Decl Erdos1192.erdos_1192.variants.renyi exists (research solved); docstring names the probabilistic method as the proof technique of the stated result; QID matches.

evidence: Asks for an infinite set A such that all elements of the sumset A+A receive the same colour.
verify_note: Decl exists; the monochromaticity condition ranges over A+A (Pointwise sumset), load-bearing; QID matches.

evidence: A monochromatic-structure-in-any-2-colouring question (Owings' problem); erdosproblems.com tags it 'ramsey theory'.
verify_note: Field-level tag: 'Ramsey theory' appears nowhere in the informal statement; the statement's concepts (sumset, 2-colouring) are separate items - generic classification, not a load-bearing mention.

evidence: Docstring: 'Erdős and Sárközy proved that such an A must have density 0' (Set.HasDensity 0).
verify_note: Decl Erdos12.erdos_12.variants.erdos_sarkozy_density_0 exists (research solved); A.HasDensity 0 and docstring 'density 0' = natural density; QID label/alias matches.
